gate1_register_mapping.description: The register used must match the margin band: margin >= 0.9 -> assertive; margin < 0.9 and ambiguous pair -> hedged_pair; margin < 0.9 otherwise -> hedged_generic. An ambiguous-pair alert with margin >= 0.9 keeps register assertive but must set needs_review=true (runtime guard; empty set on current data).
gate1_register_mapping.check: structural: compare pipeline-recorded register against case.margin and case.is_ambiguous_pair

gate2_hedged_disclosures.description: Every hedged_pair report must disclose indistinguishability, the pcap disambiguation action, and the pair probability. All three pattern groups must match somewhere in the report.

gate3_no_ordering_language.description: No hedged_pair report may contain within-pair ordering or preference language. Any single match fails the gate.

gate4_probability_consistency.description: Numeric confidence figures in the report must match the alert. Probabilities are rendered with probability_format (4 decimal places) by the prompt builder, so string comparison is exact. hedged_pair: the formatted p_pair string MUST appear at least once, and the formatted p_top1/p_top2 strings MUST NOT appear (they are the within-pair split). assertive: the formatted p_top1 string MUST appear at least once. hedged_generic: formatted p_top1 AND p_top2 must both appear.
gate4_probability_consistency.tolerance_note: String-level check by design: the pipeline formats all probabilities identically, so a mismatch means the model altered or invented a number. Exception: if p_top1/p_top2 format to the same string as p_pair (cannot happen while p_pair = p_top1 + p_top2 and both members are nonzero), the check on their absence is skipped.

notes: Gates 2-4 are text-level and machine-checkable with the regexes above; gate 1 is structural. These rules mirror prompts/register_*.md v1.0.0 - the two must be versioned together. False-positive risk accepted and documented: e.g. 'favors' could appear in benign phrasing; hedged-pair reports have no legitimate use for it.
